Class SVGImage

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.graphics.image.impl.SVGImage
All Implemented Interfaces:
Image, VectorImage
public class SVGImage extends Object implements VectorImage
An SVG image is a vector-based format.

This implementation uses native memory and has to be manually unloaded once no longer needed.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface VectorImage

    VectorImage.Vector

  • Constructor Details

    • SVGImage

      public SVGImage()

  • Method Details

    • prepare

      public void prepare()
      Description copied from interface: Image
      Prepares the image for rendering.

      This may involve:

      • Lazy decoding of compressed data
      • Rasterization of vector content
      etc. Once the image is done preparing:
      • Image.isReady() returns true
      • Image.unload() may be used to unload the data

      Idempotent: calling multiple times has no additional effect.

      Specified by:
      prepare in interface Image

    • isReady

      public boolean isReady()
      Description copied from interface: Image
      Checks whether this image is ready to be drawn.

      Returns false only if:

      • The image is still loading asynchronously
      • Required resources failed to load
      • Image.prepare() has never been called or hasn't been called after unloading
      • The image has been previously unloaded with Image.unload()
      Specified by:
      isReady in interface Image
      Returns:
      true if safe to pass to RenderCanvas.drawImage()

    • createPixelReader

      public PixelReader createPixelReader(Point2D dimensions)
      Description copied from interface: VectorImage
      Rasterizes the vector content into a pixel grid of the given dimensions and returns a pixel reader over the grid. The image must be prepared before calling this method.
      Specified by:
      createPixelReader in interface VectorImage
      Parameters:
      dimensions - the requested dimensions
      Returns:
      an object that reads this image by pixels
      See Also:
      • Image.isReady()
      • Image.prepare()

    • getIntrinsicWidth

      public double getIntrinsicWidth()
      Description copied from interface: Image
      Returns the intrinsic width of this image.

      Intrinsic Size

       Intrinsic size refers to the source's original dimensions. The actual dimensions are able to change depending on scaling and resolution. Examples:
      • Raster images have a fixed size and their intrinsic size is equal to that size;
      • Vector images have a dynamic resolution due to vector shapes being defined as paths, but the intrinsic size is typically set (e.g. <svg> element has attributes "width" & "height").
      Specified by:
      getIntrinsicWidth in interface Image
      Returns:
      the intrinsic width of this image
      See Also:
      • Image.getIntrinsicHeight()

    • getIntrinsicHeight

      public double getIntrinsicHeight()
      Description copied from interface: Image
      Returns the intrinsic height of this image. See the definition of intrinsic size.
      Specified by:
      getIntrinsicHeight in interface Image
      Returns:
      the intrinsic height of this image
      See Also:
      • Image.getIntrinsicWidth()

    • getMimeType

      public String getMimeType()
      Description copied from interface: Image
      Optional hint about the MIME type of this image.

      Examples: "image/png", "image/svg+xml"

      May return null if unknown or irrelevant (e.g. procedural images).

      Specified by:
      getMimeType in interface Image
      Returns:
      the MIME type, or null if unavailable

    • getVectors

      public List<VectorImage.Vector> getVectors()
      Description copied from interface: VectorImage
      Returns a list of vectors of this vector image. The image must be prepared before calling this method.
      Specified by:
      getVectors in interface VectorImage
      Returns:
      this image's vectors
      See Also:
      • Image.isReady()
      • Image.prepare()

    • unload

      public void unload()
      Description copied from interface: Image
      Unloads internal data associated with this image.

      After calling this method:

      • Image.isReady() returns false
      • Subsequent calls to Image.prepare() may reload the data

      Idempotent: calling multiple times has no effect.

      Specified by:
      unload in interface Image
